screeningQuestions.question: Do you hear clicking or popping when you open your mouth?
Do you grind or clench your teeth, especially at night?
Is it difficult or painful to open your mouth wide?

stretches: Gentle jaw massage (circular motions, 2 minutes)
Jaw opening exercises (slow, controlled movements)
Tongue and jaw relaxation techniques
modifications: Avoid hard or chewy foods temporarily
Use a night guard if grinding teeth
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Reduce jaw tension, soft diet
Week 3-4: Gentle jaw mobility exercises
Week 5-6: Address underlying stress factors

screeningQuestions.question: Does turning your head feel stiff or restricted?
Do you get headaches or tension at the base of your skull?
Does neck pain affect your sleep?

stretches: Gentle neck rolls (5 reps each direction)
Chin tucks (hold 5 seconds, 10 reps)
Side neck stretches (hold 30 seconds each side)
modifications: Use lighter weights for upper body exercises
Focus on posture during desk work
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Daily gentle stretching, heat therapy
Week 3-4: Add light resistance exercises
Week 5-6: Gradually return to full range movements

screeningQuestions.question: Does overhead movement feel tight or painful?
Do pushing movements (like push-ups) hurt?
Do you notice any pinching or clicking?

stretches: Doorway chest stretch (hold 30 seconds)
Cross-body shoulder stretch (hold 30 seconds each arm)
Behind-back shoulder stretch (gentle, 30 seconds)
modifications: Use resistance bands instead of weights
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Mobility and gentle stretching
Week 3-4: Light resistance band exercises
Week 5-6: Progressive return to overhead movements

screeningQuestions.question: Does overhead movement feel tight or painful?
Do pushing movements (like push-ups) hurt?
Do you notice any pinching or clicking?

stretches: Doorway chest stretch (hold 30 seconds)
Cross-body shoulder stretch (hold 30 seconds each arm)
Behind-back shoulder stretch (gentle, 30 seconds)
modifications: Use resistance bands instead of weights
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Mobility and gentle stretching
Week 3-4: Light resistance band exercises
Week 5-6: Progressive return to overhead movements

screeningQuestions.question: Do you feel tightness or pulling across your chest?
Does deep breathing feel restricted?
Do you feel rounded shoulders or forward head posture?

stretches: Doorway chest stretch (hold 30 seconds)
Wall chest stretch (hold 30 seconds each arm)
Foam rolling chest muscles (2 minutes)
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Chest stretching and posture correction
Week 3-4: Add light resistance exercises
Week 5-6: Progressive return to pushing movements

screeningQuestions.question: Do you feel rounded shoulders or hunched posture?
Do you experience tension between your shoulder blades?
Does poor posture affect your breathing?

stretches: Thoracic spine extensions (10 reps)
Doorway chest stretches (hold 30 seconds)
Cat-cow stretches focusing on upper back (10 reps)
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Improve mobility and posture awareness
Week 3-4: Strengthen postural muscles
Week 5-6: Integrate healthy movement habits

screeningQuestions.question: Do you feel stiffness when twisting or rotating your torso?
Does prolonged sitting cause mid-back aching?
Do you feel restricted when taking deep breaths?

stretches: Thoracic spine rotation (10 reps each side)
Cat-cow focusing on mid-back (10 reps)
Doorway chest stretch (hold 30 seconds)
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Improve thoracic mobility
Week 3-4: Strengthen postural muscles
Week 5-6: Integrate healthy movement patterns

screeningQuestions.question: Does sitting for long periods make it worse?
Is bending forward or lifting painful?
Do you feel stiff when you first wake up?

stretches: Cat-cow stretch (10 reps)
Knee-to-chest stretch (hold 30 seconds each leg)
Child's pose (hold 60 seconds)
modifications: Avoid heavy deadlifts and squats temporarily
Use lighter weights for bent-over exercises
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Gentle mobility, walking, avoid aggravating movements
Week 3-4: Add core strengthening, light resistance
Week 5-6: Gradually reintroduce compound movements

screeningQuestions.question: Does gripping or holding objects cause elbow pain?
Do you feel pain on the outside of your elbow (tennis elbow)?
Do you feel pain on the inside of your elbow (golfer's elbow)?

stretches: Wrist extensor stretch (hold 30 seconds each arm)
Wrist flexor stretch (hold 30 seconds each arm)
Gentle elbow range of motion exercises
modifications: Use lighter weights for arm exercises
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Rest and gentle stretching
Week 3-4: Light resistance exercises
Week 5-6: Progressive return to normal activities

screeningQuestions.question: Does gripping or holding objects cause elbow pain?
Do you feel pain on the outside of your elbow (tennis elbow)?
Do you feel pain on the inside of your elbow (golfer's elbow)?

stretches: Wrist extensor stretch (hold 30 seconds each arm)
Wrist flexor stretch (hold 30 seconds each arm)
Gentle elbow range of motion exercises
modifications: Use lighter weights for arm exercises
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Rest and gentle stretching
Week 3-4: Light resistance exercises
Week 5-6: Progressive return to normal activities

screeningQuestions.question: Do your wrists hurt during or after typing/computer work?
Do you experience numbness or tingling in your hands?
Is your grip strength weaker than usual?

stretches: Wrist circles (10 reps each direction)
Prayer stretch (hold 30 seconds)
Tendon gliding exercises (10 reps)
modifications: Take frequent breaks from computer work
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Rest and ergonomic adjustments
Week 3-4: Gentle strengthening exercises
Week 5-6: Return to normal activities with proper technique

screeningQuestions.question: Do your wrists hurt during or after typing/computer work?

Is your grip strength weaker than usual?

stretches: Wrist circles (10 reps each direction)

modifications: Take frequent breaks from computer work

screeningQuestions.question: Do your fingers feel stiff, especially in the morning?
Do your hands or fingers swell during the day?
Do you have trouble with fine motor tasks like buttoning clothes?

stretches: Finger stretches and flexion (10 reps each finger)
Thumb opposition exercises (10 reps each hand)
Hand and finger massage (2 minutes each hand)
modifications: Use adaptive tools for fine motor tasks
Take breaks during repetitive hand tasks
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Reduce inflammation and improve mobility
Week 3-4: Gentle strengthening exercises
Week 5-6: Return to normal hand function

screeningQuestions.question: Do your fingers feel stiff, especially in the morning?

stretches: Finger stretches and flexion (10 reps each finger)

Hand and finger massage (2 minutes each hand)
modifications: Use adaptive tools for fine motor tasks
Take breaks during repetitive hand tasks
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Reduce inflammation and improve mobility
Week 3-4: Gentle strengthening exercises
Week 5-6: Return to normal hand function

screeningQuestions.question: Do your hips feel tight after sitting?
Do you experience discomfort when walking long distances?
Does climbing stairs cause hip discomfort?

stretches: Hip flexor stretches (hold 30 seconds each leg)
Pigeon pose (modified, hold 45 seconds each side)
Figure-4 hip stretches (hold 30 seconds each leg)
modifications: Stand and walk regularly during long sitting periods
Use proper hip hinge technique for lifting
Focus on hip mobility before workouts
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Improve hip mobility and reduce tightness
Week 3-4: Strengthen glutes and hip stabilizers
Week 5-6: Integrate functional movement patterns

screeningQuestions.question: Do you feel pain or discomfort when sitting for long periods?
Do you feel weakness or pain when climbing stairs?
Do you have trouble sleeping on your side due to hip/glute pain?

stretches: Pigeon pose (hold 45 seconds each side)
Figure-4 stretch (hold 30 seconds each side)
Glute bridges (10-15 reps)
modifications: Use a standing desk or take frequent breaks
Add glute activation exercises before workouts
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Improve glute flexibility and reduce tightness
Week 3-4: Strengthen glutes with targeted exercises
Week 5-6: Integrate functional movement patterns

screeningQuestions.question: Do your quads feel fatigued or painful when going up stairs?
Is squatting or getting up from low positions difficult?
Do you feel quad tightness during or after physical activity?

stretches: Standing quad stretch (hold 30 seconds each leg)
Couch stretch (hold 45 seconds each leg)
Foam rolling quads (2 minutes each leg)
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Stretching and mobility work
Week 3-4: Progressive strengthening exercises
Week 5-6: Return to full squatting and jumping activities

screeningQuestions.question: Do your hamstrings feel tight when bending forward?
Does prolonged sitting make your hamstrings feel tight?
Do you feel pulling or discomfort when walking uphill?

stretches: Standing hamstring stretch (hold 30 seconds each leg)
Lying hamstring stretch (hold 30 seconds each leg)
Seated forward fold (hold 45 seconds)
modifications: Reduce running or high-intensity leg exercises
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Gentle stretching and mobility work
Week 3-4: Progressive strengthening exercises
Week 5-6: Return to full activity with proper warm-up

Does squatting or kneeling hurt?

recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Ice after activity, gentle range of motion

screeningQuestions.question: Do you experience calf cramps, especially at night?
Do your calves feel tight or painful when walking?
Do your calves feel very tight when stretching?

stretches: Standing calf stretch against wall (hold 30 seconds each leg)
Seated calf stretch with towel (hold 30 seconds each leg)
Downward dog pose (hold 45 seconds)
modifications: Gradually increase walking or running distance
Use proper footwear with heel support
Stay hydrated and maintain electrolyte balance
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Gentle stretching and massage
Week 3-4: Progressive calf strengthening
Week 5-6: Return to full activity with proper warm-up

screeningQuestions.question: Do your ankles feel stiff in the morning?
Do you experience foot pain during or after walking?
Do you feel unstable or have balance concerns?

stretches: Calf stretches against wall (hold 30 seconds each leg)
Ankle circles and flexion (10 reps each direction)
Toe stretches and curls (10 reps each foot)
recoveryPlan: Week 1-2: Reduce inflammation and improve mobility
Week 3-4: Strengthen foot and ankle muscles
Week 5-6: Enhance balance and proprioception
